source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/06_Risk Management/250116 최초 작성본/A-RMP-HNX_Risk Management Plan 250116 (Rev.0).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management plan for the HnX-P1 Portable X-ray System covering lifecycle risk activities, responsibilities, acceptability criteria, verification, and production/post-production information review.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to establish and document the manufacturer's risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure for the HnX-P1 medical device.
keyPoints: The plan applies to all stages of the life cycle of the HNX-P1.
The HnX-P1 is a battery-powered portable X-ray System designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
The device is intended for X-ray imaging and diagnosis and is not intended for mammography.
Risk acceptability uses a severity multiplied by probability risk level and divides risks into acceptable and unacceptable regions.
Production and post-production information is collected from internal and external sources and can trigger updates to the risk management file.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Han, Jimin
entities.evidence: Prepared by Han, Jimin R&D team 2024.11.05

entities.name: Kim, Myeongseop
entities.evidence: Reviewed by Kim, Myeongseop R&D manager 2024.11.05

entities.name: Hur, Namkyu
entities.evidence: Approved by Hur, Namkyu Quality Management Representative 2024.11.05

concepts.name: Risk management plan
concepts.evidence: This plan includes the following: the scope of the planned risk management activities, identifying and describing the medical device and the life cycle phases for which each element of the plan is applicable

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: If the plan changes during the life cycle of the medical device, a record of the changes shall be maintained in the risk management file.

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: We define and document the policy for determining criteria for risk acceptability

concepts.name: Overall residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: The get information method of post-production information should be collected according to the internal information and the external information.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-P1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

evidence.claim: The document is a risk management plan for the HnX-P1 Portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Management Plan Product name: Portable X-ray System Model name: HnX-P1

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.0 and the revision date is 2024.11.05.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date 2024.11.05 Revision Description Initial

evidence.claim: The plan is applicable to all stages of the HNX-P1 life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: This Risk management plan is applicable to all stages of the life cycle of the HNX-P1.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is a battery-powered portable X-ray System designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is intended to acquire X-ray images of desired parts of patient anatomy by qualified or trained physicians or technicians.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 portable X-ray System is a medical device intended for use by a qualified/trained physician or technician for the purpose of acquiring X-ray images of the desired parts of patient’s anatomy

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This system is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 classification is Class IIb.
The expected service life of the product is approximately 7 years.
Applicable national and regional regulations include Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

evidence.claim: Relevant standards include EN ISO 14971:2019 for medical device risk management.
evidence.evidenceText: EN ISO 14971:2019 Medical devices - Application of risk management to medical devices (ISO 14971:2019)

evidence.claim: Risk acceptability is calculated as severity rating multiplied by probability rating.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk level = Severity rating × Probability rating

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 to 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 to 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk Level: 1~4 Green, Acceptable Risk (A) Acceptable region Risk Level: 5~25 Red, Unacceptable Risk (U) Unacceptable region

evidence.claim: The manufacturer evaluates overall residual risk after risk control measures have been implemented and verified.
evidence.evidenceText: After all risk control measures have been implemented and verified, the manufacturer shall evaluate the overall residual risk posed by the medical device

evidence.claim: Post-production information sources include PMCF, CAPA reports, customer complaints, service records, customer satisfaction surveys, medical incident reporting, academic papers, similar-device information, regulatory and standards trends, and clinical trial information.
evidence.evidenceText: Internal (1) PMCF (Post Market Clinical Follow up) (2) Corrective and preventive actions related reports (3) Customer Complaints ... External (1) Reporting medical incidents ... (4) Establishment and revised trends in international and domestic laws / standards

evidence.claim: The Quality manager performs risk analysis for collected production and post-production information once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: Adverse event reporting is performed if necessary to the regulatory agency under applicable national or regional regulations.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority signal says FDA, but the source text does not mention FDA; identified jurisdictions are based on EU Regulation 2017/745, Korea medical device references, and FCC Sub Part C.
The source path includes 250116, while the document approval and revision history state 2024.11.05; documentDate was taken from the document body.
Several OCR or text extraction artifacts are present, including malformed spacing and wording such as 'what is currently', 'o r', and 'analysis the'.
